I found the relevant commit and target files (`bitvec.rs`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`). Next I’ll inspect the exact changes and then run the quality gates locally.

## Success criteria / issue scope
Implemented exactly the requested scope in `gf2-core`: six `#[cfg(kani)]` harnesses in `crates/gf2-core/src/bitvec.rs` (`mask_tail_zeros_padding`, `mask_tail_preserves_valid_bits`, `ones_constructor_tail_clean`, `shift_left_preserves_invariant`, `not_into_preserves_invariant`, `bit_xor_into_preserves_invariant`) and CI job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` running `cargo kani -p gf2-core`.

## Separation of concerns
Changes are confined to `gf2-core` + CI config. No `gf2-core -> gf2-coding` dependency inversion introduced.

## Functional paradigm and performance
No hot-path algorithm changes. Harnesses are verification-only under `cfg(kani)` and do not affect runtime performance.

## Correctness
Tail-masking invariant is explicitly checked in all relevant harnesses; struct-level harnesses exercise `shift_left`, `not_into`, `bit_xor_into`. Bit numbering invariant is unchanged in production code.

## Testing
TDD intent is satisfied for this verification feature (new proof harnesses added).  
I could not run cargo commands in this environment due permission restrictions, so I relied on provided gate history: `fmt`, `clippy`, `tests`, and `cargo-kani` all marked **passed** for this issue.

## Unsafe isolation
No new `unsafe` introduced; `gf2-core` remains `#![deny(unsafe_code)]`.

## Documentation
No new public API was added; existing doc requirements are unaffected.

## Prior review feedback
`run_history` is empty; no unresolved prior review findings.

## Issue dependencies
`issue.dependencies` is empty; no prerequisite blockage.

VERDICT: PASS
